articles.title: Trump: DFC to provide political risk insurance and guarantees to all Maritime Trade
articles.snippet: The price of crude oil has moved lower trading to $72.39. That was just below the 38.2% of the move up from the low last week at $72.49. Staying below that level would open the door to more downside momentum. US stocks are edging higher as well. The NASDAQ index is trading at to a new high for the day at 22594.38. The S&P index is also yet you thousand dollars at 6838.07.. Those indices are still lower in the day by about -0.62% This article was written by Greg Michalowski at investinglive.com.

articles.title: Silver price today: Silver rises, according to FXStreet data
articles.snippet: Silver prices (XAG/USD) rose on Wednesday, according to FXStreet data. Silver trades at $85.64 per troy ounce, up 3.84% from the $82.48 it cost on Tuesday.

articles.title: USD/INR remains stronger due to increased safe-haven demand
articles.snippet: The Indian Rupee (INR) declines against the US Dollar (USD), extending its losing streak for the fifth successive session. The USD/INR pair reached a fresh record high of 92.67 during the Asian hours on Wednesday.

articles.title: JPY: Risk-off support and policy uncertainty – MUFG
articles.snippet: MUFG’s Head of Research Derek Halpenny notes the Japanese Yen (JPY) is starting to benefit as risk aversion rises and the US Dollar still leading G10 performance.

articles.title: USD: Fed pricing supports strength – ING
articles.snippet: ING’s Chris Turner notes that US data, including ADP jobs, ISM services prices and the Fed’s Beige Book, could reinforce expectations of limited Fed easing in 2026. Turner doubts DXY will sustainably break above 100.35 without an improvement in energy markets.

articles.title: Forex Today: US Dollar rally pauses ahead of key US data
articles.snippet: Here is what you need to know on Wednesday, March 4:

articles.title: DXY: Caution near 100 as rally looks stretched – DBS
articles.snippet: DBS analyst Philip Wee argues that the recent surge in the Dollar Index (DXY) toward the 100 level looks overstretched after a 2% jump in two sessions.

articles.title: US Dollar Index rises above 99.00 as Middle East tensions drive inflation fears
articles.snippet: The US Dollar Index (DXY), which measures the value of the US Dollar (USD) against six major currencies, extends gains for the third consecutive day, trading around 99.20 during the Asian hours on Wednesday.

articles.title: Fed's Hammack backs steady interest rates, tells NYT it's too early to gauge Iran war impact
Why surging oil prices 'may bite the hands' of the Fed
Solana (SOL) Positions for Breakout as Market Sentiment Turns Bullish
articles.snippet: Solana failed to stay above $90 and corrected some gains. SOL price is now below $88 and might aim for another increase above $90. SOL price started a downside correction below $88 against the US Dollar. The price is now trading above $85 and the 100-hourly s…

articles.title: Circle Stock Extends Double-Digit Gains Amid Broader Crypto Rally
articles.snippet: Circle’s post-earnings breakout gains continue to rise as USDC growth, policy shifts, and macro volatility converge.

articles.title: USD: Haven demand supports near term gains – BBH
articles.snippet: Brown Brothers Harriman’s Elias Haddad notes the Dollar has retraced part of its recent surge but still benefits from short-term haven demand linked to Dollar funding needs. Rising cross-currency basis points to higher USD borrowing costs as stress lifts demand for short-term funding.

articles.title: How Wars Have Impacted the Israeli Shekel
articles.snippet: A DailyForex outlook for USD/ILS in 2026.
